name: Pancragen
subtitle: KEDW Tetrapeptide | Pancreas Bioregulator
overview: Pancragen is a Khavinson bioregulator tetrapeptide (KEDW) originally isolated from bovine pancreatic cells. Developed at Russia's St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology, it directly interacts with DNA to regulate pancreatic gene expression. Research in old rhesus monkeys demonstrated that Pancragen corrected impaired glucose tolerance, normalized insulin and C-peptide levels, and improved endocrine pancreatic function. It is considered safe and effective for age-related metabolic disturbances.
mechanism: Pancragen works through epigenetic regulation by interacting with chromatin complexes and DNA structures to modulate pancreatic gene expression. Research shows it upregulates critical transcription factors for pancreatic cell maturation including Pdx1, Pax6, Ptf1a, Foxa2, Nkx2.2, and Pax4. Its small size (4 amino acids, ~576 Da) allows it to traverse cellular membranes and interact with nuclear components including histones and DNA.

items.name: Impaired Glucose Tolerance
items.description: Corrects age-related glucose tolerance disturbances in primate studies.

items.name: Metabolic Syndrome
items.description: Reduces incidence and magnitude of metabolic syndrome.

items.name: Blood Sugar Regulation
items.description: Helps control blood sugar through pancreatic function support.

items.name: Pancreatic Cell Differentiation
items.description: Impacts differentiation of pancreatic cells during aging.

items.name: Endocrine Function
items.description: Improves endocrine function of the pancreas.

items.name: Age-Related Pancreatic Decline
items.description: Addresses age-related imbalance of pancreatic function.

items.name: Gene Expression Regulation
items.description: Directly interacts with DNA to regulate pancreatic gene expression.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. Short peptides can be absorbed orally and reach target tissues. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles.
Injectable form available for direct administration. Primate studies used intramuscular injection.

interactions.notes: Often combined in comprehensive anti-aging Khavinson protocols.
Different organ targets; can be used in comprehensive bioregulator protocols.
Part of Khavinson bioregulator family; targets different tissue.
Both affect glucose metabolism; interaction unknown.

timeline.effects: Effects persist due to epigenetic changes
Glucose tolerance and pancreatic function improvements

monitoring: Consider HbA1c testing with long-term use

references.title: Correction of Impaired Glucose Tolerance Using Tetrapeptide (Pancragen) in Old Female Rhesus Monkeys
references.keyFindings: Pancragen normalized insulin and C-peptide levels, suggesting recovery of disturbed glucose tolerance in old animals.

references.title: Tetrapeptide KEDW Interacts with DNA and Regulates Gene Expression
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: KEDW directly interacts with DNA and regulates pancreatic gene expression.

references.title: Impacts of Pancragen on Differentiation of Pancreatic Cells During Aging
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: Pancragen affects pancreatic cell differentiation during aging process.

references.title: Prospects of Using Pancragen for Metabolic Disorders in Elderly
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: Pancragen shows promise for correction of metabolic disorders in elderly people.

quickStats.storage: Capsules at room temperature; reconstituted injectable at 2-8°C refrigerated
